en.extract:
The sulphur-breasted myzomela (Myzomela jugularis), also known as the orange-breasted myzomela or orange-breasted honeyeater, is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae.


Description

The sulphur-breasted myzomela is 10 cm (4 in) long. The upperparts are black with a scarlet rump. The underparts are pale yellow-white. The curved bill and feet are black. The male has a scarlet crown.


Distribution and habitat
The sulphur-breasted myzomela is endemic to Fiji, where it is the smallest resident avian species. It breeds on all the islands except Rotuma.  It is a familiar bird in gardens as well as rural forest and mangrove habitats,  and also among coconut trees in disturbed areas.  Its natural habitats are tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical mangrove forests.


Breeding
The small nest is composed of root fibres and hidden in shrubby vegetation. A clutch of two eggs, pale pink with brown spots, is laid, and then incubated for around 14 days.


References

The '''sulphur-breasted myzomela''' ('''''Myzomela jugularis'''''), also known as the '''orange-breasted myzomela''' or '''orange-breasted honeyeater''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]].

==Description==
[[Image:Myzomela jugularis-2.jpg|left|thumb|male, Caqalai Island, Ovalau, Fiji Isles]]
The sulphur-breasted myzomela is 10 cm (4 in) long. The upperparts are black with a scarlet rump. The underparts are pale yellow-white. The curved bill and feet are black. The male has a scarlet crown.<ref name=Fijibirds>{{cite book |title=A Field Guide to Fiji Birds |last=Mercer |first=Robin|year=1967 |publisher=Government Press |location=Suva|page=22}}</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==
The sulphur-breasted myzomela is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Fiji]], where it is the smallest resident avian species. It breeds on all the islands except [[Rotuma]].  It is a familiar bird in gardens as well as rural forest and mangrove habitats,<ref name=Fijibirds/>  and also among coconut trees in disturbed areas.<ref>{{cite journal |vauthors=Steadman DW, Franklin J |year=2000 |title=A Preliminary Survey of Landbirds on Lakeba, Lau Group, Fiji |journal=Emu |volume=100 |issue=3 |pages=227–35 |doi=10.1071/MU9934 |bibcode=2000EmuAO.100..227S |s2cid=84258498 |url=http://www.publish.csiro.au/paper/MU9934.htm |access-date=2008-06-25|url-access=subscription }}</ref>  Its natural [[habitat]]s are tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical [[mangrove]] forests.

==Breeding==
The small nest is composed of root fibres and hidden in shrubby vegetation. A clutch of two eggs, pale pink with brown spots, is laid, and then incubated for around 14 days.<ref name=Fijibirds/>

[[Image:Orangebreasted Myzomela taveuni jun08.JPG|thumb|right|male, De Voeux Peak, Taveuni, Fiji Isles]]
